titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Rackstraw Downes Papers
titlestmt.author: Tina Evans and Rosemary K. J. Davis

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday January 8, 2025 at 23:26

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The collection is arranged in five series: Series I. Correpondence. Series II. Writings. Series III. Printed material. Series IV. Degrees and certificates. Series V. Travel.
Much of the of the donor's arrangement has been retained. Labeled page protectors from Downes' original binders have been copied and retained when possible.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Rodney Harry Rackstraw Downes--realist painter and writer--was born in Pembury, Kent, England in 1939. Downes studied at the Hotckiss School in Connecticut from 1957-1958, received a Bachelor of Arts in English literature from Cambridge University, as well as a Bachelor of Fine Arts (1963) and a Master in Fine Arts in painting (1964) from Yale University. After finishing his studies, Downes remained in the United States and became a citizen in 1980.
Downes is most well-known for his artworks created in the plein-air style, particularly his painted landscapes and cityscapes of Maine, far West Texas, and New York City.

Downes's writings have been published in numerous publications including the New York Times, Art News, Art in America, and others. Over the course of his career, Downes has received a Guggenheim Fellowship, a MacArthur "Genius" Fellowship, and is a member of both the Academy of Arts and Letters and the National Academy of Design.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Rackstraw Downes Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains correspondence, writings, notebooks, articles, essays, reviews, and talks, printed material, interviews, and exhibition catalogues, and other materials related to Rackstraw Downes life and work.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Rackstraw Downes Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information consult the appropriate curaor.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series are grouped in two subseries: Incoming Correspondence and Outgoing Correspondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged alphabetically by last name of author.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged alphabetically by name of recipient.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Materials in this series arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains notebooks, speeches, reviews, criticism, and other writings by Rackstraw Downes.

England Holland Belgium with Rosemarie Beck

Master of Fine Arts thesis, Yale University
Studies in art architecture and design
Watercolorist for all seasons (on Charles Burchfield)
Slide talk at Penn. for students, after visit to London, Amsterdam, Brussels
Talking to the driver: the documents of the 20th century art
Four fallacies in modern art criticism
Four fallacies in modern art criticism
Art in context: a fresh perspective
What the Sixties meant to me
Notes on landscape Alex Katz and Yvonne Jacquette
Brandeis University Fine Arts visiting committee
Real, really real, super real catalogue
Letter to the editor on Fairfield Porter and Lawrence Alloway
Letter to Editor Fairfield Porter on art and science: intellect or crank?
Henri Rosseau and the idea of the naÏve
New York Studio School fundraising appeal
Fairfield Porter, the man and ideas
What have we made of the landscape?
Fairfield Porter, the man, and the ideas
On the picture plane: notes for panel
Notes for the Fairfield Porter Night at the Poetry Project
Reinterpreting the landscape (The tenses of landscape)
Theatre for the free mind and the strong stomach
Samuel Beckett: theatre for the free mind and strong stomach
Turning the head in empirical space
Fairfield Porter the thought behind the painting
Garbage arriving at Fresh Kills in barges is hauled to the top of the landfill in athey wagons, 1990
In the High Island oil field
Water-flow monitoring installations on the Rio Grande near Presidio, TX
Pratt Institute Honorary Doctor of Fine Arts
New York Academy of Art commencement address
Jonas Mekas award for distinguished service to the arts

'Ethics' of picture plane, Cézanne's space, and the unity of action in allegorical painting
Holistic & the puristic in modern art with a proposal for a new theory of genres
Hunter Collect on tenses in representations and landscape
Memorandum from Emil Antonucci on a new program for Parsons graphics
Status of landscape artist-one of respect or dismisal?
There is a difference between what art professes and what it expresses
Those trying to explain pictures are as a rule completely mistaken
Writings by others relating to art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contain writings by others, or unidentified writings related to art collected by Rackstraw Downes.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this series arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains articles, clippings, catalogs, and other printed material, primarily related to Rackstraw Downes art.

did.unittitle: Articles, reviews, interviews, catalogues, and books about Rackstraw Downes
Articles, reviews, interviews, catalogues, and books about Rackstraw Downes
Interviews, statements, and other writings about Rackstraw Downes' art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains awards and degrees given to Rackstraw Downes.
